ops.title: CLI command architecture: shared binders, global search, flag-group constraints
ops.body:
## The rules the CLI surface is built on (Phase 2 unification, 2026-07)

- **Every flag routes through a shared binder** (`flags.go`, `args.go`, `verbs.go`): `anchorSets` (add), `anchorEdits` (edit), `anchorFilters` (list/search), `labelEdits`, `bindJSON`, `bindBody`, `bindLabels`, `bindLimit` (uniform "0 = all"), `bindRemote`, `freeText`, and `kindSpec.rmCmd`. Hand-rolling a flag one of these covers is a defect: `TestAnchoredKindsUniform` asserts note/doc/log/runbook expose byte-identical anchor/label/limit surfaces, and `testdata/vocabulary.golden` freezes the whole command tree. Regenerate goldens only deliberately (`go test ./internal/cli -run Golden -update`) and review the diff as the change log.
- **Free text is uniform**: anywhere a command takes prose, all three forms work — positional, the flag (`--body`, or the domain flag `--entry`/`--text`), and stdin via `-` — resolved by `freeText` in `args.go` (exactly-one-of; UsageError names all accepted forms).
- **Mutual exclusions are cobra flag groups**, not hand-rolled RunE checks: `MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive`/`MarkFlagsOneRequired`. `classify()` in `exit.go` pins cobra's group-error wording to exit 2 (a tripwire test guards against cobra upgrades changing the wording), and the root usage template renders a `Constraints:` block in `--help`. RunE checks remain only for conditionals cobra cannot express (e.g. `reconcile --force` requires `--from`; `supersede --by` unless `--clear`).
- **Kind-agnostic verbs live at root**: `search`, `show`, `history`, `compact`. Per-kind `search` keeps that kind's full filter set. Global search merges via `compareSearchHits` (tier desc, UpdatedAt desc, EntityID asc — deterministic, unit-pinned).
- **Filtering logic lives in `notes`, never the CLI**: list/search delegate to `notes.RunbookFilter`/`LogFilter`/`SearchFilter`. A CLI-side matcher re-implementation is drift — it happened once with runbook anchors and was folded back.
- **Status verbs come from the shared factory** (sprint/project/runbook activate/archive/complete/cancel); transition rules are enforced in `notes` (e.g. project activate accepts only archived→active).
- **Tasks are branch-scoped by design** (scalar `--branch`/`--backlog`), not full-anchor entities — do not "unify" that.
ops.when: before implementing a new CLI command or modifying command flag handling
